one_time_description: Debt adjustment on demand loans - write-down of Bridging Finance Inc. loan from ~$136M to $25M
economic_notes: First Nation has affiliates: Chief Peguis Investment Corporation, Peguis Development Corporation, Chief Peguis Investment Trust. Operates gaming, bingo, pharmacy, one-stop, and other commercial enterprises. COVID-19 impacts noted. Bridging Finance Inc. receivership claim of $113M principal plus $56M interest filed December 2022.
notes: Audit is qualified on multiple bases: (1) unable to confirm Bridging Finance Inc. demand loan balance (~$136M prior year, written down to $25M - $111M debt adjustment recorded as one-time gain); (2) unable to verify loans to affiliates CPIC, Peguis Development Corp, Chief Peguis Investment Trust; (3) accounts payable related to construction contracts not susceptible to audit verification; (4) related party receivable/payable write-offs not sufficiently evidenced; (5) Jordan's Principle entity excluded from consolidation due to unavailable records. Going concern noted due to debt servicing requirements. Demand loans include Bridging Finance Inc. at $25M (down from $136M) and RBC at $301K. 12 fully consolidated subsidiaries/unincorporated departments. Bad debt expense of $22,254,864 for CPIC receivable also recorded. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $72,069,281 + FNIH $13,235,211 + CMHC $4,290,777 + ESDC $2,819,170 + Province of Manitoba $1,255,464 + RCMP Housing $67,200 + Canadian Heritage $182,927 - Eliminations portion = approximately $93,433,439. OSR calculated as total revenue $111,564,234 minus total government transfers.
